title: Fix: inheritlabels=true if query param absent
body:
The `inheritlabels` param must default to true if query param is absent.

Fixes:  #25981

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?
None

comments.body:
Maybe simpler would be to define `InheritLabels` as `*bool` and write simple conversion function `*bool` => `types.OptionalBool`.

Something like:
```patch
diff --git a/pkg/api/handlers/compat/images_build.go b/pkg/api/handlers/compat/images_build.go
index 16b03d4340..9b9b153a3a 100644
--- a/pkg/api/handlers/compat/images_build.go
+++ b/pkg/api/handlers/compat/images_build.go
@@ -130,7 +130,7 @@ func BuildImage(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
 		IDMappingOptions        string   `schema:"idmappingoptions"`
 		IdentityLabel           bool     `schema:"identitylabel"`
 		Ignore                  bool     `schema:"ignore"`
-		InheritLabels           bool     `schema:"inheritlabels"`
+		InheritLabels           *bool    `schema:"inheritlabels"`
 		Isolation               string   `schema:"isolation"`
 		Jobs                    int      `schema:"jobs"`
 		LabelOpts               string   `schema:"labelopts"`
@@ -745,7 +745,7 @@ func BuildImage(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
 		IDMappingOptions:               &idMappingOptions,
 		IgnoreUnrecognizedInstructions: query.Ignore,
 		IgnoreFile:                     ignoreFile,
-		InheritLabels:                  types.NewOptionalBool(query.InheritLabels),
+		InheritLabels:                  boolPtrToOptionalBool(query.InheritLabels),
 		Isolation:                      isolation,
 		Jobs:                           &jobs,
 		Labels:                         labels,
@@ -953,3 +953,14 @@ func extractTarFile(anchorDir string, r *http.Request) (string, error) {
 	err = archive.Untar(r.Body, buildDir, nil)
 	return buildDir, err
 }
+
+func boolPtrToOptionalBool(ptr *bool) types.OptionalBool {
+	switch {
+	case ptr == nil:
+		return types.OptionalBoolUndefined
+	case *ptr:
+		return types.OptionalBoolTrue
+	default:
+		return types.OptionalBoolFalse
+	}
+}

```
I am opened to suggestions.
/cc @Luap99 @TomSweeneyRedHat
/cc @flouthoc
PR looks good a test in https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test/apiv2 can be added for this. Let me write a test.

@Luap99 @matejvasek I don't have permission to push on this PR so I am pasting a patch for test here.

I have created a new file so in future we can keep `build` tests seperate from `image` tests in `apiv2`.

```diff
---
 test/apiv2/90-build.at | 27 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 1 file changed, 27 insertions(+)
 create mode 100644 test/apiv2/90-build.at

diff --git a/test/apiv2/90-build.at b/test/apiv2/90-build.at
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..226ef5572
--- /dev/null
+++ b/test/apiv2/90-build.at
@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+# -*- sh -*-
+#
+# Tests for build-related endpoints
+#
+
+# test if default compat build contains labels from base image
+TMPD=$(mktemp -d podman-apiv2-test.build.XXXXXXXX)
+function cleanBuildTest() {
+    podman rmi -a -f
+    rm -rf "${TMPD}" &> /dev/null
+}
+CONTAINERFILE_TAR="${TMPD}/containerfile.tar"
+cat > $TMPD/containerfile << EOF
+FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal
+RUN echo hello
+EOF
+tar --format=posix -C $TMPD -cvf ${CONTAINERFILE_TAR} containerfile &> /dev/null
+
+t POST "/build?dockerfile=containerfile&t=labeltest" $CONTAINERFILE_TAR 200 \
+  '.aux|select(has("ID")).ID~^sha256:[0-9a-f]\{64\}$'
+
+t GET images/labeltest/json 200 \
+  .Config.Labels.license="MIT" \
+  .Config.Labels.name="fedora-minimal"
+cleanBuildTest
+
+# vim: filetype=sh
-- 
2.48.1
```
I'll apply patch!

please don't use `registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal` use $IMAGE which is `quay.io/libpod/testimage:20241011` adn adapt the labels accordingly, to avoid flakes and for test speed it is best to keep using the same image constituently

@matejvasek Applying this again on top of my previous patch, should consider @Luap99 's feedback

```diff
diff --git a/test/apiv2/90-build.at b/test/apiv2/90-build.at
index 226ef5572..8c667f8db 100644
--- a/test/apiv2/90-build.at
+++ b/test/apiv2/90-build.at
@@ -11,7 +11,7 @@ function cleanBuildTest() {
 }
 CONTAINERFILE_TAR="${TMPD}/containerfile.tar"
 cat > $TMPD/containerfile << EOF
-FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal
+FROM $IMAGE
 RUN echo hello
 EOF
 tar --format=posix -C $TMPD -cvf ${CONTAINERFILE_TAR} containerfile &> /dev/null
@@ -20,8 +20,7 @@ t POST "/build?dockerfile=containerfile&t=labeltest" $CONTAINERFILE_TAR 200 \
   '.aux|select(has("ID")).ID~^sha256:[0-9a-f]\{64\}$'
 
 t GET images/labeltest/json 200 \
-  .Config.Labels.license="MIT" \
-  .Config.Labels.name="fedora-minimal"
+  .Config.Labels.created_by="test/system/build-testimage"
 cleanBuildTest
 
 # vim: filetype=sh
```

The feature was added in buildah this is a fix for compat API before the release, I think `podman` inherits build release notes from buildah so should be fine I think. But yeah small note cannot harm.

We only have final release notes, this fixes a regression in 5.5.0-rc1 and we of course want to backport it for final. that means noting this in the release notes is kinda pointless as it was never broken for users using a normal release but @mheon can clarify if he sees things differently

/lgtm
@Honny1 wrt gpg signatures, I committed  something on behalf of @flouthoc. So at best I could sign it for myself but the commit would be still only partially verified for that reason.

wrt release notes: I would agree that this was not probably in any release. We found this only because in our project we run integration tests against nightly build each day.

The action https://github.com/knative/func/actions/workflows/test-podman-next.yaml started failing 5 days ago.

> We found this only because in our project we run integration tests against nightly build each day.

Thanks for testing (and patching!), especially nightly ones so we have a chance to find and fix things before we make releases.
